text:
The 4x100m relay is always a highlight of theOlympic Games, combining the speed of the world's top sprinters with the crucial element of teamwork in the baton changeovers.Usain Boltled Jamaica to victory in the men's event at London 2012 and Rio 2016, but Italy took Tokyo 2020 gold following his retirement.

However, Jamaica retained their crown in the women's event, previously held by the United States. Flagbearer Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce spearheaded a dominant display, which was the third-fastest time ever run in the women's event.

Athletics enthusiasts won't have to wait long to see if they can defend their titles, as the action is already underway at the Stade de France. Here's all you need to know about both the men's and women's 4x100m relays.

The heats for both women's and men's events will take place on Thursday, August 8, with the women's race starting at 10:10am UK time, followed by the men's at 10.35am. The field of 16 nations in each event will then be halved.

The final eight teams will return to the track on the evening of Friday, August 9. The women's race will start at 6:30pm, followed by the men's race at 6:47pm.



Discovery+ holds full TV rights to the Games, and fans can subscribe for just £3.99 a month until the end of 2024 to catch all the action from Paris. Both relays will be broadcast on the platform.

TheBBCwill also be broadcasting two events simultaneously. Gabby Logan will be leading their athletics coverage across BBC1, BBC2 and iPlayer.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

In terms of favourites, the United States leads the pack for both events. The women's team is priced as low as 4/11 for victory, while the men's team stands at 1/3.

Jamaica trails just behind in both categories, with Great Britain ranking third for the women's event and Italy holding the same position in the men's. However, the relay is no stranger to surprise, meaning any outfit could have their sights on gold on the day.

title: Olympic 4x100m relays: Dates, TV channel & start times for Paris races | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Dan Burnham, Tom Sunderland
description: The 4x100m relay is one of the most eagerly anticipated events on the athletics schedule at an Olympic Games - and it's no different in Paris.
